expectations.text: Handler uses Claims or WriteAccess extractor for authentication
expectations.evidence: handler.rs uses `claims: Claims` extractor in the function signature

expectations.text: Validates that new_password meets minimum length requirement
expectations.evidence: handler.rs does NOT validate new_password length — it delegates directly to perfil::cambiar_password without any length check in the handler. The transcript mentions the service does this, but the handler itself doesn't validate at the boundary.

expectations.text: Validates that current_password is not empty
expectations.evidence: handler.rs does NOT check if current_password is empty — passes it directly to the service

expectations.text: Uses argon2 verify_password (constant-time) to check current password
expectations.evidence: Delegates to perfil::cambiar_password which the transcript confirms uses argon2 verify_password internally

expectations.text: Uses argon2 hash_password to hash the new password before storing
expectations.evidence: Delegates to perfil::cambiar_password which uses auth::hash_password (argon2)

expectations.text: Does NOT log the password or hash in any tracing call
expectations.evidence: handler.rs only logs `user_id = %id` — no password values logged

expectations.text: Returns generic error message that doesn't reveal whether current_password was wrong vs user not found
expectations.evidence: Delegates to perfil::cambiar_password which returns generic error messages

expectations.text: Verifies the user is changing their own password (path id matches claims.sub)
expectations.evidence: handler.rs checks `if claims.sub != id { return Err(AppError::Forbidden); }`
